content.problemStatement:
Let's now implement dynamic programming to solve this problem.

You have a knapsack that can take upto WmaxW_{max}Wmax​ weight. 

Given NNN items, ithi^{th}ith of them having the weight WiW_iWi​ and value ViV_iVi​. You can pick some or all the items completely so that their weight does not exceed WmaxW_{max}Wmax​. 

Your task is to maximise the total value of the items you will carry in your bag.

Note that you cannot take any item in fractions; that is, you either pick it or don't.


Input Format

The first line contains a two integer NNN and WmaxW_{max}Wmax​, denoting the length of array AAA and the maximum weight you can carry.
Next NNN lines contains the description of the items.

ithi^{th}ith line contains two integers WiW_iWi​ and ViV_iVi​, denoting the Weight and Value of the ithi^{th}ith item




Output Format
Output the maximum value you can carry while not exceeding the weight limit of the knapsack.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. Each test case starts with a line containing two integers N and Wmax (1 ≤ N ≤ 1000, 1 ≤ Wmax ≤ 1000) – the number of items and the maximum weight the knapsack can hold. Then follow N lines, each containing two integers Wi and Vi (1 ≤ Wi, Vi ≤ 1000) – the weight and value of the i‑th item.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the maximum total value that can be carried without exceeding the weight limit.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Best combination is items (6,5), (6,6) and (3,5) → total weight 15, total value 17.
Take items (1,6), (2,10) and (4,15) → weight 7, value 31, which is maximal.

hidden.label: edge case – items heavier than capacity

solution.approach: We use the classic 0/1 knapsack DP. Let dp[w] be the maximum value achievable with total weight exactly w (or at most w). Initialise dp[0..Wmax] = 0. For each item (wi, vi) iterate w from Wmax down to wi and update dp[w] = max(dp[w], dp[w‑wi] + vi). The descending loop guarantees each item is used at most once. After processing all items, dp[Wmax] holds the answer for the test case. Time complexity is O(N·Wmax) per test case, space O(Wmax).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N·Wmax) per test case, i.e., O(T·N·Wmax) overall.
